State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662         322-5660              322-5901           322-6441


March 21, 1984


Cynthia Grace
West Hollywood Incorporation Committee
P.O. Box 691476
West Hollywood, CA  90069

Re:  Advice Letter No. A-84-052

Dear Ms. Grace:

     Thank you for your request for advice on the campaign
reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act.

FACTS

     You are the Treasurer for the West Hollywood Incorporation
Committee (WHIC). WHIC, in an effort to incorporate West
Hollywood, has filed an application with the Local Agency
Formation Commission (LAFCO). LAFCO will hold hearings and
approve or disapprove the application. If it is approved, the
issue will go before the Board of Supervisors which will decide
whether to submit the matter to the voters. WHIC has filed its
Statement of Organization and one campaign statement.

QUESTION PRESENTED

     What are WHIC's campaign filing requirements?

DISCUSSION

     Although WHIC has filed one campaign statement, it actually
has no reporting responsibilities until the incorporation
proposal becomes a "measure." This will occur when the Board of
Supervisors orders the proposal placed on the ballot. (See the
enclosed copy of the Fontana Opinion, No. 75-162, February 4,
1976.) In the meantime, you have properly filed WHIC's
Statement of Organization and you should maintain records of
receipts and expenditures for WHIC's future campaign reporting
responsibilities.

Cynthia Grace
March 21, 1984
Page 2


Once the incorporation proposal becomes a "measure," WHIC
will be a committee primarily formed to support the measure.
WHIC's filing deadline will depend upon the date of the
election. (See the "1984 Information Manual on the Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act," pgs. 6-9,
Sections B-D.) The first campaign statement filed will cover
the period beginning with the day after the closing date of the
last statement filed by your committee. It will include all
contributions received, and expenditures made, for the purpose
of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the
voters for the passage of the measure, even if the contributions
and expenditures occurred prior to the proposal becoming a
"measure." (See the enclosed copies of 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Sections 18215 and 18225.) You will not be required to report
any receipts or payments that were made for the purpose of
influencing the LAFCO or the Board of Supervisors.

If the Board of Supervisors disapproves the request for
incorporation and WHIC decides to attempt to qualify the
proposal as an initiative, please contact our office again, as
WHIC will incur additional reporting responsibilities.

If you have any additional questions, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division

Enclosure

cityhood
West Hollywood

March Pong Du
Secretary of State
1230 J St.
Political Reform Division
Sacramento, Calif.

FPIC

MAR 7 12:42 PM '84

March 1, 1984
ID # 840252

Dear Secretary of State,
    I am writing on behalf of the West Hollywood Incorporation
Committee, of which I am Treasurer, to request clarification with
respect to the filing dates for campaign statements. Our state-
ment of organization (form 410) was filed on Feb. 2, 1984. Our
first campaign statement (form 420) was filed on Feb. 9; it
covered the period from the first reportable transaction to the
time of qualification.

    The 1983 Information Manual, published by the FPPC, states,
at page 9, Section E, that committees formed to qualify a measure
for the ballotmust file a campaign statement either 35 days after
the deadline for filing petitions, or 35 days after the notice
of qualification.

    WHIC is seeking to incorporate West Hollywood under procedures
set forth in the Municipal Organization Act. Our application to the
Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO) includes a petition.
(Gov. Code 35100) However, the petition alone, even if sufficient,
does not automatically qualify the measure for the ballot.

    There is no deadline for the submission of the petition,
either in state statute, or in the administrative procedures of
the local agency. Furthermore, we are not, by virtue of the
application, automatically guaranteed an election date.

    Lengthy hearings will be held before LAFCO and the Board of
Supervisors. (Attached for your information is a synopsis of the
statutory time frame for holding these procedures.) LAFCO has
broad towers with respect to whether to approve or deny the
application. The Board of Supervisors has discretion as to
whether to submit the question to the voters for confirmation.
An election date, if any, will be determined by the sequence of
hearing dates before LAFCO and the Board of Supervisors. There
is no "notice of qualification." However, the order of confirmation
by the Board of Supervisors (Govt. Code 35257) may be roughly
analogous.

    Since WHIC does not quite fall into the five categories of
committees described in your manual, please advise me when I
should file future campaign statements.

                                                    (2/2) [illegible]

                            Yours truly,


                            Cynthia Grace
                            Attorney at Law

PETITION

Circulation period                6 mos. from date of          G.C. 35116
                                  first signature

Submittal to LAFCO              Within 60 days from          G.C. 35116
                                  date of last signature

Examination by LAFCO            Within 30 days from          G.C. 35118
Issuance of certifi-            date petition is filed
cate of sufficiency

If insufficient:                Have 15 days from date       G.C. 35118
                                  of certificate of
                                  insufficiency in which
                                  to file supplemental
                                  petition; and within
                                  10 days after receiving
                                  supplemental petition,
                                  LAFCO must examine and
                                  certify results.


LAFCO PROCEEDINGS

Hearing                         must be within 90 days       G.C. 54793
                                  from date "certificate
                                  of filing" is issued
                                  (LAFCO meets 2nd & 4th
                                  Weds. each month)

Notice                          Must be published,           G.C. 54793
                                  posted and mailed at
                                  least 15 days prior
                                  to date of hearing

Continuance                     Hearing can be continued     G.C. 54797
                                  up to 70 days from
                                  original date of hearing.

Determination                   Within 35 days after     -   G.C. 54797
                                  conclusion of hearing,
                                  LAFCO must adopt resolution
                                  making its determination.

(If denied by LAFCO, proceedings go no further.  No new pro-
posal involving any of the same territory may be filed with
LAFCO for at least one year from date of disapproval without
the consent of the commission. G.C. 54799)


BOARD OF SUPERVISORS

Resolution              Within 35 days from date of  G.C. 35201
                        LAFCO approval, Board
                        must adopt resolution
                        initiating proceedings &
                        setting date for hearing

Hearing                 Can't be less than 30       G.C. 35250
                        nor more than 45 days
                        from date of resolution
                        initiating proceedings

Notice                  Must be mailed, published    G. C. 35251-52
                        and posted at least 15
                        days prior to hearing

Continuance             Hearing can be continued    G.C. 35253
                        up to 60 days from
                        original hearing date

Determinations          Not more than 30 days       G. C. 35257
                        after conclusion of
                        hearing, Board must
                        adopt resolution
                        either terminating
                        proceedings (if 50% or
                        more voter protest) or
                        calling election (if
                        less than 50% voter
                        protest).

Election                Resolution calling          E.C. 23302
                        election must be
                        adopted at least
                        88 days prior to
                        date of election.

Regular election
dates

2nd Tues., April of
each even-numbered year;
1st Tues. after 1st Mon.,
March of each odd-numbered
year; 1st Tues. after 1st
Mon. June each year; and
1st Tues. after 1st Mon.
November each year.

E.C. 2500

Election results

Registrar shall prepare
certified statement of
results of the election
& submit it to the Board
within 28 days of the
election.

E.C. 17088

Final Resolution*

After canvass of election
returns, Board must adopt
resolution either terminating proceedings (if
majority vote against) or
confirming its order of
incorporation (if majority
favor).

G.C. 35261-63

COMPLETION

Certificate of
Completion

If election successful,
Board sends LAFCO certified
copy of final resolution;
LAFCO executes and records
Certificate of Completion.
Incorportion becomes effective
on date of recordation.

G.C. 35354

(If election fails, no new proposal of the same or substantially the same territory may be filed with LAFCO for at
least two years from date of resolution terminating proceedings, unless such provisions are waived by LAFCO. G.C. 35264)

*No time specified in Code.
